title: sql-splitter generate config
description: Schema for the `sql-splitter generate` command's `kind: model` and `kind: overrides` YAML documents. Add `# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://sql-splitter.dev/schemas/generate-config.schema.json` as the first line of a config file for editor validation and completion.

ChildDistribution.description:
The fan-out distribution used to allocate a relationship child's rows
across its parents.

ColumnRule.description:
A single column's generation rule: an optional semantic annotation, an
optional generator owner, and an ordered modifier pipeline.

ColumnRuleOverride.description: A partial patch for one column's generation rule.

FingerprintPolicy.description: How strictly a model requires the source fingerprint to match.

max_seconds.description: Maximum offset after the source, in seconds; defaults to 86400.

min_seconds.description: Minimum offset after the source, in seconds; defaults to 1.

source.description: The sibling column to generate relative to.

max_seconds.description: Maximum offset before the source, in seconds; defaults to 86400.

min_seconds.description: Minimum offset before the source, in seconds; defaults to 1.

source.description: The sibling column to generate relative to.

values.description: The non-empty list of values to pick from.

value.description: The constant value to emit; SQL NULL when omitted.

source.description: The sibling column to copy the value from.

length.description: Number of characters drawn after the prefix; defaults to 32.

prefix.description: Literal text prepended before the random characters; defaults to `sk_`.

length.description: Number of characters drawn; defaults to 48.

prefix.description: Literal text prepended before the random characters.

length.description: Number of characters drawn; defaults to 32.

prefix.description: Literal text prepended before the random characters.

max.description: Inclusive upper bound in seconds; defaults to 86400 (one day).

min.description: Inclusive lower bound in seconds; defaults to 0.

bins.description: Sorted, non-overlapping `{ min, max, count }` bins.

scale.description: Decimal places for decimal columns; defaults to 0.

length.description: Number of hex characters; defaults to 64 (SHA-256-shaped).
Number of characters drawn; defaults to 21.

prefix.description: Literal text prepended before the random characters.

length.description: Number of characters drawn; defaults to 32.

prefix.description: Literal text prepended before the random characters.

value.description: The document to emit; defaults to `{}`.

mu.description: Mean of the underlying normal (log space).

scale.description: Decimal places for decimal columns; defaults to 0.

sigma.description: Std-dev of the underlying normal (finite, non-negative).

start.description: The value at row 0; defaults to 0.

scale.description: Decimal places for decimal columns; defaults to 0.

values.description: The non-empty list of `{ value, weight }` entries (or bare values).

distribution.description: Parent-assignment distribution: uniform, sequential, weighted, or observed.

null_rate.description: Fraction (0.0..=1.0) of children whose key is NULL (nullable columns only).

relationship.description: Name of the declared relationship this key follows.

distribution.description: Parent-assignment distribution: uniform, sequential, weighted, or observed.

null_rate.description: Fraction (0.0..=1.0) of children whose key is NULL (nullable columns only).

relationship.description: Name of the declared relationship this key follows.

start.description: The first value emitted; defaults to 0.

step.description: The increment applied after each row; defaults to 1.

parts.description: A list of literal strings and `{ field: <name> }` references.

choices.description: The non-empty list of `{ value, weight }` entries.

InferenceMode.description:
Whether columns without an explicit owner may fall back to schema-based
heuristics, or must always be explicit.

InsertMode.description: How PostgreSQL row output is rendered.

ModelDefaults.description:
Inherited table/column behavior defaults.

Both `output` and `defaults` are spec-optional on a `kind: model`
document (see the "Top-level fields" table); a model that omits
`defaults` gets [`InferenceMode::Disabled`], matching what
`--emit-config` always writes explicitly.

ModelKind.description: Marker type accepting only the literal `kind: model` tag.

template.description: A string containing the literal `{value}` placeholder.

max_length.description: Truncate to this many Unicode scalars afterward, if set.
Truncate to this many Unicode scalars afterward, if set.
The number of Unicode scalars to keep.

max_attempts.description: Retries allowed before resolving via on_exhaustion; defaults to 10.

max_tracked.description: Maximum number of distinct values remembered; defaults to 1,000,000. Reaching this budget resolves via on_exhaustion, the same as a collision.

OutputMode.description: Whether rendering emits DDL, rows, or both.

OutputModel.description:
Dialect and renderer defaults (`output:` in the complete model example).

A model that omits `output` entirely preserves the source/base dialect
(`dialect: None`); see [`ModelDefaults`] for why this field is
`#[serde(default)]` on [`SyntheticModel`].

OutputOverride.description: A partial patch for `output:`; present fields replace the base value.

OverridesKind.description: Marker type accepting only the literal `kind: overrides` tag.

child_columns.description: Maps child quantity, unit price, line total, and optional discount and tax roles.

children.description: Name of the line-item table coordinated by this parent planner.

columns.description: Maps parent subtotal, total, and optional discount, tax, and shipping roles.

currency_scale.description: Decimal scale shared by every parent and child money column.

relationship.description: Relationship on the child table that references the parent table.

rounding.description: Exact residual allocation mode: largest_remainder, last_line, or bankers.

shipping.description: Fixed shipping amount in minor or major currency units.

unit_price.description: Per-line unit-price range in minor or major currency units.

columns.description: Maps name and optional extension, MIME type, size, and hash roles to columns.

hash_kind.description: Synthetic digest shape: md5, sha1, sha256, or sha512.

size.description: Minimum and maximum generated byte size.

bounds.description: Optional minimum and maximum latitude and longitude bounding box.

columns.description: Maps latitude and longitude roles to decimal columns.

precision.description: Number of decimal places, from 0 through 9.

columns.description: Maps the parent role to the nullable self-reference column.

key.description: Explicit dense key start and step, overriding relationship-derived values.

max_branching.description: Maximum number of direct children per parent; omitted means unbounded.

relationship.description: Self-relationship used to derive the referenced dense key recipe.

root_ratio.description: Fraction of rows generated as roots with a null parent.

columns.description: Maps the left and right roles to the junction table's foreign-key columns.

left_relationship.description: Relationship that supplies the left dense key domain.

right_relationship.description: Relationship that supplies the right dense key domain.

columns.description: Maps the polymorphic type and id roles to columns.

targets.description: Weighted target tables with optional type labels and id-column overrides.

columns.description: Maps tenant and parent roles to the child table's columns.

relationship.description: Relationship that supplies the parent dense key domain.

columns.description: Maps the required start, end, and duration roles plus an optional open role to columns.

duration.description: Configures the fixed, uniform, normal, histogram, or observed duration draw.

end_inclusive.description: Whether the end timestamp is inclusive of the final duration unit.

open_probability.description: Probability that a row is open and therefore has no end timestamp.

open_value.description: Boolean value written to the optional open column for an open row.

start.description: Configures the range, observed range, or monotonic start timestamp draw.

timezone.description: Timestamp rendering zone: preserve, utc, or an IANA timezone name.

columns.description: Maps the status role and each lifecycle state to its timestamp column.

start.description: Configures the first lifecycle timestamp range or sequence.

step.description: Configures the non-negative delay between lifecycle states.

weights.description: Terminal-state weights in the same order as states.

columns.description: Maps the required deleted_at role and optional is_deleted role to columns.

deleted_range.description: Configures the range or monotonic sequence for deletion timestamps.

deletion_probability.description: Probability that a generated row is marked deleted.

columns.description: Maps created_at, updated_at, and optional trailing timestamp roles to columns.

created.description: Configures the created timestamp range or monotonic sequence.

other_delay.description: Configures the delay from creation for additional timestamp roles.

update_delay.description: Configures the non-negative delay from creation to update.

active_statuses.description: Status labels available to active and not-started rows.

columns.description: Maps total and optional counter, status, and completion roles to columns.

completed_statuses.description: Status labels available to completed rows.

partition.description: Selects exact or allow_unclassified counter partitioning.

progress.description: Selects a fixed lifecycle state or a weighted state mixture.

success_ratio.description: Share of classified processed rows assigned to succeeded rather than failed.

total.description: Configures the fixed or uniform total-count draw.

unclassified_ratio.description: Share of processed rows left unclassified under allow_unclassified partitioning.

PortableCheckConstraint.description: A CHECK constraint, with its raw SQL expression preserved verbatim.

type.description: Shorthand alias for `source_type`; `family` is derived automatically when omitted.

PortableIndex.description:
An index definition (not necessarily unique; see
[`PortableUniqueConstraint`] for UNIQUE constraints).

PortableRelationship.description:
A declared foreign-key relationship to another table, by name (not
resolved to an ID, since a `PortableSchema` stands alone from the
`Schema` it was built from).

PortableTableOverride.description:
A partial patch for `schema:`. Overrides cannot redefine DDL structure
(see the design's "source, model, and overrides merge predictably"
section), so this only covers non-structural annotations.

PortableUniqueConstraint.description: A table-level UNIQUE constraint, covering one or more columns.

ProfileInference.description: The inference explanation attached to a [`ProfileMetadata`] entry.

ProfileMetadata.description:
Removable evidence recorded for one profiled column
(`profiles."table.column"` in the complete model example). Deleting a
model's `profiles` map cannot change generation.

RelationshipModel.description:
A declared generation relationship to another table.

This covers the common named foreign-key shape used throughout the
complete model example. Self-referential, polymorphic, and shaped
(tree/junction) relationships are a documented follow-up; see the
task report for why they are out of scope here.

RowsKind.description:
The tag for [`RowsOverride::kind`]; standalone since an override may
carry only a subset of the fields a complete [`super::model::RowsModel`]
requires (for example, `{ kind: observed, scale: 0.01 }`).

RowsModel.description: How many rows a table produces, and how those rows are derived.

RowsOverride.description:
A partial patch for `rows:`. Unlike [`super::model::RowsModel`], a count
or distribution may be omitted when the source/base model supplies it.

SourceModel.description:
Provenance and fingerprint policy for the source dump a model was
derived from (`source:` in the complete model example).

SqlTypeFamily.description:
Coarse-grained classification of a SQL column type, independent of the
source dialect's exact type name. Generation strategies key off this
instead of re-deriving it from `source_type`.

SyntheticModel.description: A complete, self-contained synthetic data model.

$schema.description:
Editor-only schema pointer (e.g. `# yaml-language-server: $schema=...`
promoted into the document body, or a literal `$schema:` key). Purely
documentation metadata: recognized so an editor-annotated document
doesn't trip `deny_unknown_fields`, never read by the parser.

SyntheticOverrides.description: A partial patch document applied on top of a source dump or base model.

TableModel.description: A single table's complete generation rules.

seed.description:
Tri-state seed: omitted inherits the model seed, `null` opts out of
determinism, and an integer pins an independent seed. Schema'd as
`Option<u64>` (the same shape [`deserialize_table_seed`] reads)
since [`TableSeed`] itself has no direct YAML representation.

TableOverride.description: A partial patch for one table.
